name: Tiramisu
instructions:
1. In a medium saucepan, whisk together the egg yolks and sugar until well blended and slightly pale. Gradually whisk in the milk until smooth. Place the saucepan over medium heat and cook, stirring constantly with a wooden spoon or whisk, until the mixture thickens slightly and comes to a gentle boil. This custard base should coat the back of a spoon. Boil gently for 1 minute to cook out the raw egg taste, then immediately remove from heat. Allow to cool slightly, then cover the surface with plastic wrap to prevent a skin from forming. Chill in the refrigerator for 1 hour until completely cold.

2. In a medium bowl, beat the cream and vanilla extract with an electric mixer on medium-high speed until stiff peaks form. The cream should hold its shape when the beaters are lifted. Set aside. Remove the chilled egg yolk mixture from the refrigerator. Add the mascarpone cheese and whisk vigorously until the mixture is smooth and creamy with no lumps.

3. In a shallow dish, combine the brewed coffee and rum, stirring to mix. Split the ladyfinger cookies in half lengthwise to create thinner layers. Working with a few at a time, quickly dip each ladyfinger half into the coffee mixture, allowing it to absorb the liquid but not become soggy. Arrange half of the soaked ladyfingers in a single layer in the bottom of a serving dish, fitting them snugly together.

4. Spread half of the mascarpone mixture evenly over the layer of ladyfingers, smoothing with a spatula. Gently spread half of the whipped cream on top of the mascarpone layer. Repeat the layers once more: soaked ladyfingers, remaining mascarpone mixture, and remaining whipped cream. Smooth the top layer.

5. Using a fine-mesh sieve or sifter, generously sprinkle cocoa powder over the entire top surface of the tiramisu, creating an even dusting.

6. Cover the dish with plastic wrap and refrigerate until the dessert is set and the flavors have melded together, 4 to 6 hours or overnight for best results. The ladyfingers will soften and the layers will meld into a creamy, cohesive dessert. Serve chilled.
simpleInstructions:
1. Cook egg yolks with sugar and milk until thickened, then chill for an hour.

2. Whip cream with vanilla, then mix mascarpone into chilled yolk mixture.

3. Dip split ladyfingers in coffee-rum mixture and layer in dish.

4. Alternate layers of mascarpone mixture and whipped cream.

5. Dust generously with cocoa powder and chill 4-6 hours before serving.
